1990_23_08-India-Mumbai-ISKCON_Chowpatty-SB_Class-st2-vr01

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Beginning of Samudra Manthan

Event Date

23 Aug 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai, Venue Name

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Swapnil Khire

Completed

18 Apr 2021

Source Audio

https://radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-068-First take the speck out of your eye (Mumbai)-BG_8_7_4-dt_90_08_23.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

SB 8.7.1, 8.7.2 and 8.7.3

Tags – Places

NA

Tags – Pastimes

Samudra Manthan & arrangement of Demons and Demigods places for churning

Tags – Persons

The Personality of Godhead, Demons & Demigods

Tags – Themes

Faith, Trust on God, Spiritual Master

Lecture Transcription

śrī-śuka uvāca

te nāga-rājam āmantrya

phala-bhāgena vāsukim

parivīya girau tasmin

netram abdhiṁ mudānvitāḥ

ārebhire surā yattā

amṛtārthe kurūdvaha

[SB 8.7.1]

TRANSLATION

Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O best of the Kurus, Mahārāja Parīkṣit, the demigods and demons summoned Vāsuki, king of the serpents, requesting him to come and promising to give him a share of the nectar. They coiled Vāsuki around Mandara Mountain as a churning rope, and with great pleasure they endeavored to produce nectar by churning the ocean of milk.

hariḥ purastāj jagṛhe

pūrvaṁ devās tato ’bhavan

[SB 8.7.2]

TRANSLATION

The Personality of Godhead, Ajita, grasped the front portion of the snake, and then the demigods followed.

tan naicchan daitya-patayo

mahā-puruṣa-ceṣṭitam

na gṛhṇīmo vayaṁ puccham

aher aṅgam amaṅgalam

svādhyāya-śruta-sampannāḥ

prakhyātā janma-karmabhiḥ

[SB 8.7.3]

TRANSLATION

The leaders of the demons thought it unwise to hold the tail, the inauspicious portion of the snake. Instead, they wanted to hold the front, which had been taken by the Supreme Personality of Godhead and the demigods, because that portion was auspicious and glorious. Thus the demons, on the plea that they were all highly advanced students of Vedic knowledge and were all famous for their birth and activities, protested that they wanted to hold the front of the snake.

PURPOT

The demons thought that the front of the snake was auspicious and that catching hold of that portion would be more chivalrous. Moreover, Daityas must always do the opposite of the demigods. That is their nature. We have actually seen this in relation to our Krishna consciousness movement. We are advocating cow protection and encouraging people to drink more milk and eat palatable preparations made of milk, but the demons, just to protest such proposals, are claiming that they are advanced in scientific knowledge, as described here by the words svādhyāya-śruta-sampannāḥ. They say that according to their scientific way, they have discovered that milk is dangerous and that the beef obtained by killing cows is very nutritious. This difference of opinion will always continue. Indeed, it has existed since days of yore. Millions of years ago, there was the same competition. The demons, as a result of their so-called Vedic study, preferred to hold the side of the snake near the mouth. The Supreme Personality of Godhead thought it wise to catch hold of the dangerous part of the snake and allow the demons to hold the tail, which was not dangerous, but because of a competitive desire, the demons thought it wise to hold the snake near the mouth. If the demigods were going to drink poison, the demons would resolve, "Why should we not share the poison and die gloriously by drinking it?"

In regard to the words svādhyāya-śruta-sampannāḥ prakhyātā janma-karmabhiḥ, another question may be raised. If one is actually educated in Vedic knowledge, is famous for performing prescribed activities and has been born in a great aristocratic family, why should he be called a demon? The answer is that one may be highly educated and may have been born in an aristocratic family, but if he is godless, if he does not listen to the instructions of God, then he is a demon. There are many examples in history of men like Hiranyakasipu, Ravana and Kamsa who were well educated, who were born in aristocratic families and who were very powerful and chivalrous in fighting, but who, because of deriding the Supreme Personality of Godhead, were called Rakshasas, or demons. One may be very well educated, but if he has no sense of Krishna consciousness, no obedience to the Supreme Lord, he is a demon. That is described by the Lord Himself in Bhagavad-Gita (7.15):

na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ

prapadyante narādhamāḥ

māyayāpahṛta-jṣānā

āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ

"Those miscreants who are grossly foolish, lowest among mankind, whose knowledge is stolen by illusion, and who partake of the atheistic nature of demons, do not surrender unto Me." Āsuraṁ bhāvam refers to not accepting the existence of God or the transcendental instructions of the Personality of Godhead. Bhagavad-Gita clearly consists of transcendental instructions imparted directly by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. But asuras, instead of accepting these instructions directly, make commentaries according to their own whimsical ways and mislead everyone, without profit even for themselves. One should therefore be very careful of demoniac, godless persons. According to the words of Lord Krishna, even if a godless demon is very well educated, he must be considered a mūḍha, narādhama and māyayāpahṛta jṣāna.

Everything we see is according to our particular consciousness

World is compared to a mirror. Everything we see around us is a reflection of our own state of consciousness. Everyone sees the world through different eyes, "One man’s food is another man’s poison". If one is envious in heart, one will see every living being as being as being envious. One will see how everyone must be also motivated by envy just like himself. And therefore, such a person cannot trust with his heart of anyone. If one's heart has been accumulated by volumes of lustful desires, he sees that everyone must be motivated similarly and if one has a very good and pure heart, it is very difficult for such a person to see anything bad in anyone. In his innocence he is naturally seeing that everyone must also be like this. The extreme case is the heart of maha bhagavat, where such a person sees that everyone is serving Krsna better than him. So, we must understand that everything we see is not necessary how it is, but it is it is according to our particular consciousness. Srila Prabhupada used the example that if you are wearing yellow colored glasses, everything you see is yellow, if you are wearing red colored glasses or rose-colored glasses everything you see will be of a rose color. If you are wearing the green colored glasses everything appears to be green. So, the reality has nothing to do with any of these shades or colors. They are simply a reflection of what is within your own heart.

So here we find the demons, how they are so suspicious, because they are always crooked, because they are always motivated by their own sense gratification, because they are willing to cheat, to lie, to steal for their own sense gratification, they are willing for the purpose of satisfying number one or oneself, they have no scroups (00:10:10 - 00:10:20 could not get the word) or rules of morality. Therefore, they are thinking that everyone must be like them. Here we find Ajita, the Supreme Personality of Godhead who is “Suhrdam sarva bhutanam” who is the best well-wishing friend of everyone. He is so kind to enter the arena of this great churning of the ocean of milk for the purpose of preserving what the demigods and demons have and caring what they lack.

Snake and human beings, both are envious

We see the front portion of snake is very very dangerous. Is anyone afraid of the tail of a serpent? What can the tail do to you? But the front portion is very very vicious, with its sharp fangs, its bifurcated tongue and it is very very strongly poisonous venom. The front portion of the snake is the most deadly off all creatures on earth. Besides human beings, there is no creature on earth that kills more innocent beings than the serpent. In India, I once saw a study by the National Geographic. It said the largest single cause of death in India was snakebites and in many other countries in south East Asia this is also true. Snakes are very very dangerous because they are envious by nature. Any other animal will never kill unnecessarily. A lion, a tiger, they are not so dangerous because they will only kill if they genuinely feel that their life is being threatened, to defend themselves or if they are hungry, and for the purpose of survival they have no other alternative. Otherwise they never kill, they are peaceful. But the snake simply because they are envious, they will strike even when there is no danger and even when they are not hungry they will just strike for the sake of causing pain and death to another. Of course there is only one other creature so envious as the snake and that is the human being who has an envious mind. He will also for no reason save and accept to satisfy his envy engage in violent activity. So the snake and the envious human beings are the two most vicious creatures on earth.

Krsna & Spiritual Master are well-wishers, Everyone else in the world is cheater

So the Supreme Personality of Godhead Sri Ajita, knowing that this was a very very important function to churn the ocean of milk and knowing how dangerous the head of snake was, with its sharp teeth and its poison, he assumed the position of holding that very head himself, just to relieve others of the danger. So he was actually the well wisher, the friend but as I say, an envious person feels that everyone must also be motivated by envy, a greedy person sees that everyone also must be motivated by greed, a doubtful person feels that everyone also must have doubt. So therefore, they were thinking that why the Supreme Lord is taking the head of the snake, why the demigods are on that side, that means the head is better than the tail. We want what you have because we know you will only accept that position if it is detrimental to us. You are only accepting that position because you know that, that will be the cause of our defeat. Because after all we would only take a position if it meant your defeat. Therefore you can take a position if it is meant for our defeat. Therefore they revolted, we will not have any part of this unless you give us the head, because we know the head is more advantageous otherwise you would not accept it.

So here the Supreme Personality of Godhead is actually acting in such a way to protect them, but they do not trust him. They are thinking that he is a cheater. If you are a cheater, you think everyone is a cheater and, in this world, basically everyone is a cheater. Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur said this is the world of the cheaters and the cheated. He uses the example of how this world is going on. The example is that sometimes people think that if my daughter marries a sadhu then she will produce very very auspicious offspring and our family dynasty will go on in a very very wonderful manner. So, therefore when the sadhus come to the house the father arranges the beautiful daughter to serve him in such a way that he will never want to leave in and he will want to marry her daughter and in this way they will have the first class sadhu as the son in law. So, in other words they are trying to cheat the innocent sadhus but the sadhus the low class man, who are crooks and criminals and of the lowest birth, they know that this is the plan. So they will disguise themselves as the sadhus and go to the very highly aristocratic peoples' houses and the young girl, thinking him as sadhu, will treat him very nicely and they will say yes yes yes, I will off course marry you and I will give you all blessings. And this way the family gets a crooked, a criminal and low-class sinful man as the son in law. This is an example of how this world is the world of cheaters and cheated and because everyone is essentially cheating and trying to exploit us for sense gratification, people become very distrustful. Who can you really trust in this world, who can you reveal your mind to without being cheated, who can you put your faith in without being cheated? Our friends, generally they are our friends because they are enjoying some sense gratification by our association. Therefore they are simply exploiting us for what they can get out of us. Our family members are generally the same way. When a father can no longer provide for the family, so often, the family wants nothing to do with him, they simply criticize him. When a son is a rascal and he is not fulfilling, this rascal by the calculation of family, he is not fulfilling what their conception of sense gratification is then they are no longer kind to that son or daughter and the people all around us, the whole society, they are simply politician and so forth, they simply want our support for their sense gratification. How many are actually concerned with the people? Practically none. This is the world where everyone is simply trying to cheat us because everyone is motivated by satisfying themselves, their own senses, their own mind. Everyone is after their own prestige. And if you get in their way, you are discarded. Everyone is naturally motivated for themselves, for my family, for my nation. Therefore, trust is one of the most difficult and rare commodities within this entire world. Real trust, to actually be able to put your faith and your hope in the motivations of another.

So, our guru Maharaj, A C Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, he used to again and again explain to us, that if you want to make proper spiritual advancement, you simply have to accept within your heart that your Spiritual Master has not come to cheat you. Everyone else has come to cheat you, even so called people in the mode of goodness who are very pious who are concerned with your physical and mental condition, by pampering so much and showing so much concern for your physical and mental condition, they are simply perpetuating your bodily conception of life. And they are cheating you, of the opportunity of realization of the eternal self and this is the best type of person in this world. How many people really care about your body and your mind? Most people are simply concerned with their body and their mind and they will care about yours as long as that is satisfying them. But in the mode of goodness somebody may actually & genuinely be concerned with your body and your mind and your welfare but by doing this, they are cheating you of the opportunity of self-realization because they are perpetuating your bodily conception of life by putting your consciousness there. So factually everyone but a genuine sadhu is a cheater. Because the truth is, as we have read this morning, that we are all part of Krsna, we are in him we are his and, one is honest is one who presents the truth. If one is not presenting the truth, whatever his motivation may be, even if he is a rare rare soul, who is not selfishly motivated, still he is cheating you because he is presenting to you a type of sympathy, which is based on a lie, on an illusion that is I am this body. The truth is that we are all part of God. Therefore only one who is trying through selfless compassion to elevate our consciousness to that conception, that person is a man of truth and therefore that person is actually is qualified to be trusted. He is a real friend. “Suhrdam sarva bhutanam”. Krsna is the supreme friend and one who is representing Krsna; he can be trusted because he is the representative of our true friend. So the demons, they are thinking that Krsna, Ajita, he must be thinking just like us. Therefore they rejected the whole idea of taking part unless they could take what the demigods had.

If we simply have faith that our spiritual master has not come to cheat us but he has come to give us out of compassion truth and life then with that trust, with that faith, we can advance, we can prosper in our spirituality. Without that faith, without that trust it is very very difficult. Therefore it is said

yasya deve parā bhaktir

yathā deve tathā gurau

tasyaite kathitā hy arthāḥ

prakāśante mahātmanaḥ

[Śvetāśvatara Upaniṣad 6.23]

One who has implicit faith in Guru and Krsna, all revealed knowledge of the Vedas is revealed to us from within our heart. Without faith in Guru and without faith in God and the scriptures, however much we study, however much we become scholarly in scriptural knowledge, however many austerities we may perform, the real truth will never be revealed from within our heart. Faith is the most important building block and the foundation of our spiritual advancement. It is said in the new testament that faith can build, it can move mountains. And the business of cleaning our heart, is the business of moving mountains and mountains and mountains of impurities outside from our life. There are mountains of pride and lust and envy and greed within us and all those mountains must be moved out. It is only through faith that it is possible. Faith comes by hearing. Therefore sravanam is the first and the most important of all the nine processes of devotional service. There are nine processes, sravanam, kirtanam, smaran, vandanam, pad sevanam, dasya re, pujanam, sakhi jana, atma nivedana, but unless one has faith in the other eight processes then it will have very little effect. Therefore unless one is sufficiently hearing from the proper sources, one cannot perform any of the nine processes of devotional service because if you are chanting, if you are remembering, if you are offering pooja, but you are offering these services without faith, they will have little effect. Therefore the basis of our spiritual life is to hear from sadhus, the words of God, the words of the scriptures and by regularly hearing that faith awakens in our heart.

Faith can be awaken by losing faith in ourselves

How does faith awaken? It begins where we have to lose faith in ourselves. This is the completely opposite conception of the materialistic psychiatrists and psychologists. They are very much concerned that people have a balanced and stable mind by having a high faith in his esteem for their own position, for their own integrity. But we find in Bhagavad-Gita, faith (00:28:00 - 00:28:10 there was no voice) if for his own esteem, his own opinion, he was not qualified to hear the word of God. Krsna simply said silently listening to him ramble on and on and on. It wasn't until Arjuna came to that critical state where he lost all faith in himself, he lost all faith in all his high learning, in all his capabilities, he was trembling, he was pale white, his mouth was dry, he was shedding tears. To use your pride is very painful. It is just the physical manifestation of what happens when a person looses all pride in himself. In that condition he was able to open his heart with all humility and enquire submissively. Krsna you please instruct me, I will do what you want. And it was at that time that he could hear and through hearing from Krsna, real faith awakened within his heart and by that faith Arjuna was able to do what nobody else could do, because he had faith in the words of Krsna and he had faith that if I simply execute the desire of Krsna then everything will be auspicious. He was able to conquer the entire kuru dynasty. We see when the Spiritual Master asks us to do something, we have to have faith that if I simply execute his order, it will be all auspicious. If we are thinking but may be I should not do this, may be its not the correct thing after all according to my logical study of what is best for me or what is best for the world, it doesn't make sense. So therefore, how can I put my heart in doing something like this? I will do something else. I will do it so slowly that sooner or later my Guru Maharaj will understand the truth and will change his mind and will not be of so much effect or bad effect before too much damage is done. A devotee who actually has faith, faith beyond material reason, such a person can be empowered by God.

Srila Prabhupada would so often emphasize that in Srimad Bhagwatam, it is said the moon is more far away than the sun and of course so many devotees; they could not tolerate hearing all this. According to all the scientific research, the sun is much farther than the moon. Now factually according to the Vedas in the Surya Siddhanta it is said that the Sun is farther than the moon. In the Srimad Bhagwatam it is said that the moon is farther than the sun and they are both perfectly correct. From the point of view of measuring in terms of latitude, the sun is farther. From the point of view of longitude the moon is farther. It depends on what perspective you are looking at it. It is like if the earth is here, the sun is here, the moon is here, if you are looking from this point of view the sun is farther if you are looking from this point of view the moon is farther. So Shukdev Goswami is not seeing the way we see. He is seeing from the point of God who is seeing from above. He is seeing from a completely different perspective then we are. So when Shukdev Goswami says like this, so many devotees are saying what is going on here. How can we accept this scripture, all of our textbooks, all of our scientists, they are all saying something different. So devotees were leaving on this basis. Now all Srila Prabhupada had do is to say I forget it, but now every opportunity he can, in dozens and dozens of purport, where there is no need, by any practical consideration, he keeps reminding us that the moon is farther. Why did he do that? What is this really has to do with becoming Krsna conscious? At one point devotees were complaining but Prabhupada said what difference does it make whether the moon is farther or sun is farther. The problem of life is not that. The problem is birth, old age, disease, death. Can the scientist solve that problem? But the sadhus and scriptures can. So that is the real problem. But why did he emphasize this? Its not a problem at all? He emphasized it because he understood that the greatest impediment in spiritual advancement is to put more faith in the speculative reasoning of your own sense perception and the sense perception of others over the word of God, the word of the scriptures.

We see through our ears, we do not see through our eyes. We see through the authority of God's words because God’s words are speaking of the absolute truth but our sense perception, our mental and sensual perception of this world is all based on our limited conditioned experience of an illusion that we have been conditioned to consider true since time immemorial. So what faith can you have in anything you think or see?

So therefore, the first principle of knowledge is, you have to, you have to loose all your faith in yourself and as soon as you loose all your faith in yourself then you can put complete faith in the word of God. When I say loosing faith in yourself that means loosing faith in your sense perception of what is reality. After all, if we were so knowledgeable to understand, what is truth why are we in such illusion. Why are we suffering so much in this world? Why can we not solve our problems? Therefore a devotee of the Lord has faith that the scriptures were not written to cheat us. The scriptures were spoken by God to enlighten us and to liberate us for all sufferings and the words of bonafide sadhus who are representing the scriptures, who are the embodiments of the scriptures, they have not come to cheat us. They are simply coming to give, give, give, give us liberation from all the problems, all the sufferings, all the turmoil of life and if we have this faith, we can open our hearts to accept the mercy of God, but if you do not have that faith then you cannot fully open your heart, therefore you cannot accept the mercy of the Lord.

How to kill the greatest demon doubt

So doubt is a demon. Arjuna declares in Bhagavad-Gita that the greatest demon is doubt. Krsna himself said in Gita that for the doubting soul there can be no happiness either in this world or the next and Arjuna approaches Krsna as Madhusudana. Krsna you are the killer of great demon, where the greatest demon is this doubt within my heart. Please kill the demon of doubt. And through his words Krsna killed the demon of doubt. Similarly through the words of Krsna which are coming down through disciplic succession, through the mouth of great saints, they have the potency to kill the demons of doubt within our heart. As long as we are haunted by this demon of doubt, we cannot open up the doors to our heart to allow Krsna to come in, to allow his mercy to enter. Doubt is like a watchdog at our heart and whenever the mercy of Krsna is coming through the words of the Guru, the sadhus and the shastra, this demon of doubt is always barking, bauh bauh bauh, chasing it away and all when we hear in our heart the barking of this demoniac dog of doubt. So we have to allow Krsna to kill this demon of doubt. Only then can our door open to receive the mercy of God.

So here we see how the demons, their best friend and well-wisher, they are doubting him because they are thinking Krsna must be like us. We are only considering what is best for ourselves at the cost of anyone else’s life. Therefore everyone must be like this. A suspicious person is even suspicious of the most compassionate and loving person. Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati Thakur used to say, I only see faults in others because I am honey combed by hundred thousand faults within my heart. Shishupal saw faults in Krsna. He could not stop chanting the faults of Krsna because he was so full of faults that even in the most purest form of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he could only see reflection of his own fault within his own mind. So therefore we should not (Jai Sri Radha Gopinath) fall prey to this great trap of maya.

The three gate which lead to hell

We should know that whatever false we see in others are due the false within ourselves. If we were pure we would see purity in others. If we were divine we would see the divinity in others but if we are envious we will see envy in others. If we are greedy we will see greed in others. Lord Jesus Christ said before pointing the little speck in another person's eye you should take the tree out from your own eye. We all are very anxious to point out the fault in others but no one is at all anxious to find out the faults within himself and factually the only faults are within ourselves.

vidyā-vinaya-sampanne
brāhmaṇe gavi hastini
śuni caiva śva-pāke ca
paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ

[Bg 5.18]

[The humble sages, by virtue of true knowledge, see with equal vision a learned and gentle brāhmaṇa, a cow, an elephant, a dog and a dog-eater (outcaste).]

If you learn how to see God within your own heart, you will see God within heart of everyone. But if all you see within your own heart is lust, anger and greed then you will simply see lust, anger, greed in everyone's heart.

Krsna says in Gita

tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ

dvāraṁ nāśanam ātmanaḥ

kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas

tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet

[Bg 16.21]

[There are three gates leading to this hell – lust, anger and greed. Every sane man should give these up, for they lead to the degradation of the soul.]

Whenever we find faults in others, whenever we doubt the pure words of the great souls we are simply degrading our own souls. Therefore these anarthas, these bad qualities must be given up. How to give them up? We have to want to give them up.

Faith in Krsna & Guru being Well Wisher

Everything is based on our desire. We have to look for the good in others. We have to look and pray for faith in Guru and Krsna. We have to accept those things, which are favorable for the development of our faith & trust in God and his representatives, and we have to reject anything, which is unfavorable for the development of faith and trust in Krsna and His representative. Material propaganda will always create distrust. Therefore sadhu satsanga, the association of saintly persons who are constantly glorifying the Names, the pastimes of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, that is most important because by hearing from them, faith and trust is awaken within our heart and the demoniac tendencies are destroyed but by associating, by hearing from the propaganda of such demoniac persons, when we speak of demoniac, we are speaking of Godless persons, persons who have no faith and trust, persons who are always suspicious because their motivations are always selfishly motivated, they are on the bodily concept of life which is a lie, which is a illusion. We must avoid submissively hearing from such sources. We must accept the position as an austerity of being the servant of the servant. As an austerity we have to stop serving our own mind and our own senses and their dictations and we must accept the divine words of higher authorities.

Through this process we gradually cultivate faith. We gradually destroy the demon of doubt. Just yesterday one person had a great crisis in his life and this person was very much depressed. So the immediate thing that we tried to do is find some material solution to pacify our mind and our heart and find some material solution to make things right. As for everyone was telling us what we should do, but that is not a solution, but even if you solve that immediate problem what is the guarantee that tomorrow a worst crisis will not come in your life and the time of death is the worst crisis imaginable and you are simply day by day approaching that crisis and what other crisis come in between, you do not know but your life will simply be one big crisis in the end and so many other crisis in between. So material arrangements never solve the problem but if you have faith that if I surrender to Krsna, he will protect me, if you have faith that Krsna is the Supreme Controller, he is the Supreme Protector and everything he does is perfect, if you have faith that if I turn to Krsna with humility then he will do whatever is required to make my life all auspicious. However pierced to my mind and senses, it is all auspicious, if I am sincerely trying to be his servant and the servant of the servant of his servant. If you want to be the servant, and the servant of his servant, if you have that faith, what anxiety should you be in? You should now be guaranteed that the supreme goal of life is yours in the end.

man-manā bhava mad-bhakto

mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru

mām evaiṣyasi satyaṁ te

pratijāne priyo ’si me

[Bg 18.65]

[Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.]

Krsna says in Gita always think of me, become my devotee, worship me and offer your homage to me. In this way Krsna says you will come to me without fail, I promise you this. You are my friend; God has promised we will come to him without fail. If we simply endeavor to always think of him, become his devotee, offer homage unto him, why should we doubt the word of God? As long as we know that we are sincerely trying to humbly follow the words of Krsna and Guru, whatever the crisis is, whatever the magnitude of our immediate sufferings may be, we have infinite hope because we are sure, we are certain, that God's promise will always be fulfilled.

Srila Prabhupada used to tell us so many times, have faith in Krsna; surrender to Krsna. There is no need for anxiety. Anxiety comes when there is lack of faith. Where there is faith, faith is like the sun, the darkness of all of these negative elements of material existence cannot remain. So let us put aside our faith in the illusory perceptions of our own mind and senses and the mind and senses of others and let us put our faith only in those who are purely and strictly repeating the words of God as it is coming down through disciplic succession. Let us chant the Holy Names with this faith and destroy the anarthas, and unwanted conditionings within our own heart and then we will see the good within all. When Krsna or His representatives come to help us, let us have faith and trust that they are our well-wisher. Srila Prabhupada would end every letter, “Your well wisher”. If we simply have faith that Prabhupad is our ever well-wisher then there will be no impediments in our spiritual progress. And in the future verses tomorrow or next day we will be reading about what the result is of the demons’ doubts in the motivation of Krsna. Krsna was trying to help them, they did not trust. What the results of their distrust would be, is a wonderful fantastic story that we will start reading tomorrow and a great lesson is to be learnt. Is there any question?

Q&A Transcription

Q1. (00:51:00 - 00:51:40 Question not heard properly)

Answer: By hearing from the right source, by hearing from God, through the scriptures, through the lips of great soul. If we hear regularly,

naṣṭa-prāyeṣv abhadreṣu

nityaṁ bhāgavata-sevayā

bhagavaty uttama-śloke

bhaktir bhavati naiṣṭhikī

[SB 1.2.18]

[By regular attendance in classes on the Bhāgavatam and by rendering of service to the pure devotee, all that is troublesome to the heart is almost completely destroyed, and loving service unto the Personality of Godhead, who is praised with transcendental songs, is established as an irrevocable fact.]

But we have to hear with a desire to hear. We have to hear like a hungry man taking his meal, not like a sleepy man. If you are sleeping during class, you are not hearing anything. But if we are anxious to hear, anxious to digest every drop of every word that is coming, Krsna will reveal Himself as you approach Him. These words are non different than him. They are transcendental sound vibrations. And simply by hearing submissively with an eager heart these words, your faith will grow. But when you talk about faith being substantiated by the results what that does mean? That means your faith is still based on sense perception. That is why generally great souls will not perform miracles because the foolish and unintelligent people, they have faith in someone who can perform miracles, so called miracles, that means their sense perception is seeing that this person can do something extraordinary.

Now it is very difficult for such a person's faith to grow beyond a certain point because he will always require some evidence based on sense perception to confirm whatever he has faith in and ultimately God cannot be perceived through sense perception. So therefore, generally except on rare occasions real acharyas, they do not perform miracles but they preach the truth because if they can convince the followers to have faith in the word of god, then there is no limitation to how their faith can grow but if your faith is based on seeing some miracle your faith will always be limited to how much you can perceive with your sense perception. You will always need re-confirmation through sense perception. Therefore Lord Jesus performed so many miracles because he was preaching to a class of people who were so lowly and materialistic but when he came to one town and he spoke and they required no miracles, he was most pleased with them. He said these people are more intelligent. They have simply accepted the word of god without all this physical science. So depending on the physical science or the results, will lead to a great impediment because ultimately the realization of God is beyond any physical science and beyond any result that you can perceive with your senses, it it not?

ataḥ śrī-kṛṣṇa-nāmādi

na bhaved grāhyam indriyaiḥ

sevonmukhe hi jihvādau

svayam eva sphuraty adaḥ

[SB 1.2.18]

[“ ‘Therefore material senses cannot appreciate Kṛṣṇa’s holy name, form, qualities and pastimes. When a conditioned soul is awakened to Kṛṣṇa consciousness and renders service by using his tongue to chant the Lord’s holy name and taste the remnants of the Lord’s food, the tongue is purified, and one gradually comes to understand who Kṛṣṇa really is.’]

You can never perceive God with your physical mind or senses. Only when your mind and senses become completely spiritualized and purified through devotional service, then you perceive the Lord. Therefore the highest, the highest quality of faith is based on hearing, is developed by hearing not by physical results or physical science. So we should very very attentively with a submissive heart approach to the word of god and those who are representing the word of God and those scriptures, which are representing the word of God.

Is there any other question? Shyamananda you are convinced?

Srila Prabhupada, I remember one time, the leaders of ISKCON were all together, then Srila Prabhupada looked at all of them, each one individually. This is years and years ago. This is in 1972. He looked at them and said “Are you convinced, Are you convinced? Are you convinced, that Krsna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, that by surrendering to him everything will be obtained. Are you convinced?” He said with such intensity that you could not lie to him & everyone was just looking and they couldn't say yes, they couldn't say no and he just looked piercingly at each and every devotee right through his eyes into his soul and asked "Are you convinced". The devotees couldn’t say yes or no. They were afraid to say no and they could not honestly say yes and then Srila Prabhupada said that if we are simply convinced with the amount of devotees we have right now, we can take over the world in 18 days and establish Krsna Consciousness as a supreme rule and order of the earth. But because you are not convinced of the words of your Guru, therefore you cannot be empowered. Conviction, faith is the foundation of our spiritual life. Because when a great soul piercingly asks you, you cannot lie. All you can do is that you must reveal the truth of your heart that we must be convinced. That conviction comes by hearing daily by the submissive and eager heart and by rendering service. You have to practice what you hear. The realization of that conviction takes place when you hear submissively and you apply it to your life.

Thank you very much

Hare Krishna

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
